capture.content:
# Combined querysets (union/intersection/difference) — compiler bypasses the outer query

- `QuerySet.union()/intersection()/difference()` set `Query.combinator` and stash the component queries in `Query.combined_queries` (`django/db/models/sql/query.py`). At compile time `SQLCompiler.as_sql` branches to `get_combinator_sql` (`django/db/models/sql/compiler.py`), which builds SQL purely from `self.query.combined_queries` — the OUTER query's WHERE clause (and most other outer state) is never consulted.
- Consequence: any Query method that mutates outer state expecting it to change results silently no-ops on combined queries unless it also recurses into `combined_queries`. Canonical example: `Query.set_empty()` (~line 1778) only added a `NothingNode` to the outer WHERE, so `QuerySet.none()` on a union returned ALL rows — dangerous in practice because `ModelMultipleChoiceField` calls `.none()` on empty form submission, so an empty submit added every object in the union. Fix: make `set_empty()` propagate to each query in `combined_queries` (and have `is_empty()` detect the NothingNode) — single file, `django/db/models/sql/query.py`.
- Combined queries are a recurring bug surface: many QuerySet operations are restricted or subtly broken after `.union()`. When something "has no effect after union()", first check whether the relevant state lives on the outer Query while `get_combinator_sql` never reads it.

## Localization lesson
For "X silently does nothing on a combined/union queryset" bugs, fix belongs at the Query mutation method in `django/db/models/sql/query.py` (propagate the mutation into `combined_queries`), NOT in the compiler's `get_combinator_sql`. The agent's 1-file localization (query.py, `set_empty`) matched the real fix footprint exactly. This extends the fix-where-state-is-produced rule from the model-string-references dossier: the compiler is a consumer of Query state; fix where that state is produced/mutated.
